full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: implementing the Auxiliary algorithm (Alg 3) from the KLR paper. when Zt=1 the update uses h'(z) not l'(z) right? for h(z)=ln(gamma+e^-z) whats the derivative?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Derived h'(z) = -e^{-z}/(gamma + e^{-z}). Had to get this right or the whole algorithm would be wrong.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Manually differentiated h(z) = ln(gamma + e^{-z}) and confirmed the derivative matches.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: my kernel computation is really slow rn. im looping over each support vector individually to compute k(x_new, sv_i). is there a vectorized way??
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Use np.sum((X-x)**2, axis=1) or scipy cdist. Wrapped into gaussian_kernel_vector and gaussian_kernel_matrix functions.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Tested both functions on small arrays — outputs match manual computation.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: for the projection onto Omega — do I rescale ALL the alpha values at once? and how do I compute ||f||_H when f is a sum of kernel functions?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: ||f||^2 = alpha^T @ K @ alpha where K is the gram matrix. If norm exceeds R, scale all alphas by R/||f||. Simple scalar rescaling.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Verified by computing norm before and after projection on a toy example — norm correctly clipped to R.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: confused about the averaged classifier. do I average the predictions f_t(x) over all t or average the weights? getting weird accuracy numbers
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Average the predictions not the weights. cumulative_test_pred += f_test each step, then /T at end. Fixed a bug in my code.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: After fixing, accuracy matched expected range. Checked paper Algorithm 1 line 9 to confirm.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: paper selects R via 5-fold CV. how do I do cross-validation for R in an online learning setting? feels weird to CV an online algo
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Use sklearn KFold, run the full online algo on 4 folds, evaluate on held-out fold. Made a lighter CV function and saved best_R to data/best_R.npy.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Ran CV and got R=20 as best. Checked that eta = 20/sqrt(455) = 0.94 < 2 as required by Theorem 3.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: plot accuracy vs number of SVs like figure 3 in the paper. multiple lines for different gamma values on same axes
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Suggested sv_history on x-axis, acc_history on y-axis with one line per gamma. Used this structure for the plot.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Plot qualitatively matches Figure 3 in the paper — higher gamma curves reach same accuracy with fewer SVs.

top_5_prompts.why_important: Had to get this gradient right or the whole algorithm would be wrong.
